strengths: Demonstrated hands-on experience with agentic workflows and AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators.
Proven track record in building event-driven, scalable systems (e.g., GISCollective, Dunnhumby microservices) with real-time data handling.
Strong engineering leadership through open-source project ownership, workshops, and cross-functional team mentoring over 15+ years.
concerns: No explicit evidence of multi-agent AI systems deployed in regulated fintech environments or with financial decisioning consequences.
Lack of clear leadership experience scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+ in a venture-backed scale-up context.
Limited visibility into experience with financial infrastructure (credit systems, working capital, compliance) or hybrid outsourced engineering governance.
response.justification: The candidate brings strong technical depth in event-driven systems, agentic tooling, and full-stack engineering, with notable open-source and mentorship contributions, but lacks demonstrable experience in regulated fintech domains and executive-scale team leadership required for this CTO role.
